page.content: The table presents an analysis of various class names and their respective sequence metrics. The class name CNL comprises 310 sequences, with an average sequence length of 1,014, an alignment length of 5,050, an identity of 16.3%, and identical sites at 0.3%. The RLP class contains 107 sequences, boasting an average sequence length of 651, an alignment length of 1,734, an identity of 17.1%, and identical sites at 0.0%.

page.content: The TNL class has 103 sequences, featuring an average sequence length of 1,146, an alignment length of 3,717, an identity of 25.6%, and identical sites at 0.1%. The TIR class consists of 82 sequences with an average sequence length of 226, an alignment length of 801, an identity of 29.3%, and identical sites at 0.5%. The NBS-LRR class includes 45 sequences, averaging 831 in sequence length, with an alignment length of 2,750, an identity of 15.4%, and identical sites at 0.4%. The NBS class has 37 sequences, with an average sequence length of 361, an alignment length of 1,070, an identity of 14.5%, and identical sites at 0.1%. The CC-NBS class contains 30 sequences, averaging 395 in sequence length, with an alignment length of 804, an identity of 16.4%, and identical sites at 0.1%. The TIR-NBS class features 22 sequences, with an average sequence length of 502, an alignment length of 833, an identity of 31.8%, and identical sites at 1.0%. The PTO-like class consists of 16 sequences, averaging 561 in sequence length, with an alignment length of 921, an identity of 35.7%, and identical sites at 6.0%. Finally, the MLO-like class includes 10 sequences, averaging 427 in sequence length, with an alignment length of 622, an identity of 55.5%, and identical sites at 14.1%.
